set in G whose image under f is P
  rcases hP with ⟨Q, hQ, hQ'⟩

    have hF : Q ∈ F := h hG
    exact ⟨Q, hF, Set.Subset.refl Q⟩
  -- Obtain the set R in F that is a subset of Q

  -- Show that the image of R under f is a subset of P
  have hP' : f '' R ⊆ P := by

      <;> simp [image]

      exact hR'
    have hP'' : f '' Q ⊆ P := by
      rw [hQ'']
      <;> simp
    exact Set.Subset.trans hR'' hP''
  -- Conclude that P is in the image of F under f

have h_main : map f F ≤ map f G := by
    -- Use the property that if F ≤ G, then the image of F under f is less than or equal to the image of G under f.
    exact Filter.map_mono h
  -- The result follows directly from the established inequality.
  exact h_main

have h₁ : F.map f ≤ G.map f := by
    apply Filter.map_mono
    <;> assumption
  exact h₁

have h_main : F.map f ≤ G.map f := by
    -- Use the monotonicity property of the map operation on filters
    exact Filter.map_mono h
  -- The result follows directly from the monotonicity property
  exact h_main
